stories.title: TOMY Boon PIVOT Toddler Tower Recalled — 106K Units

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
⚠️ RECALL ALERT: TOMY has recalled ~106,000 Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Towers for tip-over and fall risk. Stop use immediately. Contact TOMY for a free repair kit mailed directly to you. [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Does your toddler use a Boon PIVOT Tower? TOMY recalled ~106,000 units July 16 for tip-over risk. Store it away from your child and get the free stabilizing repair kit from TOMY. [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you have a Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower at home, here's what you need to know right now.

TOMY has recalled approximately 106,000 of these toddler towers in the United States — the same ones sold at Target, Amazon, and Babylist from December 2022 through earlier this year. The hazard is serious: the tower can shift or lean while a child is on it, creating a tip-over and fall risk. There have been 11 reports of shifting or leaning, and zero injuries so far. The goal is to keep it that way.

The remedy is straightforward: stop using the tower immediately, store it somewhere your child can't reach, and contact TOMY for a free stabilizing repair kit they'll mail directly to you.

Not sure if yours is affected? Look for gray and white colorways, "boon" printed on the top rail, and dimensions of approximately 22 inches wide by 23 inches deep by 35 inches tall. The towers sold for $149.99 and were available at Target.com, Amazon.com, and Babylist.com.

More details and the TOMY contact link are in the article below. Have you seen this recall come through your feeds today?

---
facebook.image_caption: ⚠️ TOMY has recalled ~106,000 Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Towers for tip-over and fall risk. Stop use now and contact TOMY for a free repair kit — mailed directly to you. Full details linked.

article.headline: TOMY Recalls Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower

research.facts:
TOMY Boon PIVOT Toddler Tower Recall
**Source confidence:** HIGH (confirmed via multiple regional news outlets + CPSC URL pattern consistent with official recall)
**Note:** CPSC.gov returned 403 during direct fetch; recall number (26-XXX) could not be confirmed from primary source. Recommend verifying at cpsc.gov before final publish. All key facts confirmed via 8+ independent regional news station reports (WSOC-TV, etc.).
**Verified facts (HIGH):**
- Product name: Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower / Kitchen Step Stool (brand: Boon, manufacturer: TOMY)
- Units recalled: approximately 106,000 in the U.S.; plus ~2,100 Canada, ~403 Europe/Middle East
- Date of recall announcement: July 16, 2026
- Retailers: Target.com, Amazon.com, Babylist.com
- Sale period: December 2022 – May/June 2026
- Price: $149.99
- Colors: gray and white
- Approximate dimensions: 22" wide × 23" deep × 35" tall; metal and plastic construction; "boon" printed on top rail
- Hazard: tower is unstable and can shift or lean, posing tip-over and fall risk to toddlers
- Incidents/injuries: 11 reports of shifting or leaning; zero injuries reported
- Remedy: Stop using immediately; store away from children; contact TOMY for a free stabilizing repair kit mailed directly to consumers
- CPSC URL pattern: cpsc.gov/Recalls/2026/TOMY-Recalls-Boon-PIVOT-Collapsible-Toddler-Tower-Kitchen-Step-Stools-Due-to-Risk-of-Serious-Injury-and-Death-from-Tip-Over-and-Fall-Hazards
**NOT confirmed (LOW — do not use):**
- Exact CPSC recall number (e.g., "Recall #26-XXX") — could not verify from primary source due to 403
**Source:** WSOC-TV wire/regional news; cpsc.gov URL pattern

stories.title: AAP Calls for Universal Free School Meals — July 2026 Policy Statement

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The AAP just called for universal free school meals for every student. Their new research shows school meals improve diet quality and do NOT cause obesity. What COS families need to know: [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Pediatricians are officially pushing for universal free school meals. HR.8798, the Universal School Meals Program Act, is in Congress now. See what the AAP policy means for COS families. [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
The American Academy of Pediatrics just published something worth every COS parent's attention. 🍎

In a new formal policy statement in the journal Pediatrics, the AAP is officially calling for universal free school meals for every student in the U.S. — regardless of family income. This isn't a suggestion; it's a full policy position from the AAP's Council on School Health and Committee on Nutrition, published in July 2026.

Here's the part that stood out: the AAP research directly addresses a common parent concern — that school lunches contribute to childhood obesity. The evidence says otherwise. School meal participation is actually associated with improved diet quality, and the research shows it does not increase obesity risk.

The context matters for Colorado Springs families. D11 already runs one of the region's largest free summer meals programs, and D11, D20, and D49 students all return in August. The legislation that would make universal school meals permanent — HR.8798, the Universal School Meals Program Act — is currently in Congress.

We broke down exactly what the AAP said, what the research shows, and how COS families can engage. Link is below. What do you think: should free school meals be universal for every student?

---
facebook.image_caption: The AAP is officially calling for universal free school meals for every student 🍎 — and new research shows school meals improve diet quality without increasing obesity risk. Full story linked.

article.headline: Pediatricians Now Officially Back Free School Meals for Every Child

research.facts:
AAP Universal Free School Meals Policy (July 2026)
**Source confidence:** HIGH (confirmed from AAP newsroom, Pediatrics journal, congress.gov)
**Verified facts (HIGH):**
- Document type: Formal AAP Policy Statement
- Title: "The Role of the Pediatrician to Promote Effective Approaches for Child and Adolescent Nutrition in Schools"
- Issuing bodies: AAP Council on School Health and Committee on Nutrition
- Publication: July 2026 issue of Pediatrics
- Core recommendation: Universal access to free school meals for all students, regardless of income
- Finding: Participating in school meal programs is associated with improved diet quality
- Finding: School meal participation does NOT increase obesity risk (directly addresses a common parent misconception)
- Finding: School breakfast participation remains "lower than optimal" — AAP urges pediatricians to promote breakfast access
- Historical context: COVID-era universal free meal waivers sharply increased participation and reduced food insecurity; AAP wants this made permanent
- Action items for pediatricians: advocate through school wellness councils, community partnerships, and direct policy advocacy
- Related legislation: HR.8798, Universal School Meals Program Act of 2026 (Sanders-led), pending in Congress
- Companion research: "Statewide Universal School Meals Policies and Food Insecurity: Difference-in-Differences Analysis" (same journal issue; confirms policy adoption reduces food insecurity)
- Primary source: publications.aap.org/pediatrics/article/doi/10.1542/peds.2026-077404/207880/
- AAP news: publications.aap.org/aapnews/news/35299/AAP-policy-calls-for-universal-school-meals-to
- Legislation: congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/8798/text
**COS local angle (MEDIUM — reliable secondary sources):**
- D11 already operates a large free summer meals program (covered July 9 pipeline)
- El Paso County has significant child food-insecurity rates
- D11/D20/D49 return in August — school year meal access is an immediately relevant question for COS families
**NOT to claim (LOW):**
- Specific El Paso County food insecurity statistics — use general language, not a specific percentage

stories.title: COS Backpack Bash — Free Backpacks at 4 Locations, July 25 and Aug. 1

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Free backpacks for COS students — no sign-up required. The Backpack Bash hits four locations on July 25 and Aug. 1. Students must be present. Supplies are limited, so arrive early. [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Backpack Bash locations: July 25 — Sand Creek HS East + PPUW Family Success Center (9 AM–noon). Aug. 1 — Mitchell HS + Hillside Community Center (9 AM–noon). Free backpacks while supplies last. [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Back-to-school season is closer than it feels, COS families — and there's a free backpack event you'll want on your calendar. 🎒

The Backpack Bash is back for 2026, organized by Mercy's Gate, COSILoveYou, and more than 70 community partners. They're giving away free backpacks to students at four locations across Colorado Springs on two Saturdays before school starts. No registration required.

Here are the dates and locations. On Saturday, July 25, the event runs at Sand Creek High School East (7005 N. Carefree Circle) and the PPUW Family Success Center (1520 Verde Drive), both from 9 AM to noon. On Saturday, Aug. 1, it moves to Mitchell High School and Hillside Community Center, again from 9 AM to noon.

A few things to know before you go: students must be present to receive a backpack, supplies are limited, and early arrival is strongly recommended. Local family resource vendors, Kona Ice, and a coffee truck will also be on site.

D49 students start Aug. 4, D11 starts Aug. 12, and D20 starts Aug. 13 — so both weekends fall right in the window when families need this most. Full location details are in the article below. Are you planning to attend one of these?

---
facebook.image_caption: Free backpacks for COS students at four locations on July 25 and Aug. 1 🎒 No sign-up needed — just show up early. Students must be present. Full details linked.

stories.title: Hello Kitty Cafe Truck at Promenade Shops at Briargate — Tomorrow Only

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Hello Kitty Cafe Truck stops at The Promenade Shops at Briargate TODAY — 10 AM to 7 PM. Macarons, merch, and the 10th Anniversary glass mug. Credit cards only, no cash. 🎀 [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your kiddos are fans, today's the day. 🎀

The Hello Kitty Cafe Truck is making a one-day stop at The Promenade Shops at Briargate in Colorado Springs — and it runs until 7 PM tonight. That means you've got most of the day to swing by, but given how popular these stops tend to get, earlier is always better.

On the menu: Hello Kitty macarons, branded merchandise, and the featured item for this stop — a 10th Anniversary glass mug with a glitter handle. Keep two things in mind: payment is credit card only (no cash accepted), and everything is available while supplies last.

No ticket, no reservation — just show up. Full details are in the article below. Who's heading to Briargate today?

---

stories.title: Cat Fest Colorado Comes to Colorado Springs — July 25, Norris Penrose

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Cat Fest Colorado comes to COS on July 25 at Norris Penrose — cat show, adoption booths, costume contest, and more. Tickets start at $20 (under 5 FREE). Limited VIP tickets available. 🐱 [URL]

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your family has a cat lover in it — or a child who has been asking for a cat — July 25 just got a lot more interesting. 🐱

Cat Fest Colorado is coming to Norris Penrose Event Center on July 25, running from 11 AM to 5 PM. The lineup includes a TICA Cat Show put on by the Yellow Rose Cat Club, breed meet-and-greets, a costume contest, face painters, an Egyptian Cat Temple, and educational presentations. If adoption is on your radar, Look What The Cat Brought In rescue will have adoption booths on site.

Tickets are $20 for general admission, and kids under five get in free. The Family 4-Pack is $72. Military tickets are $15. VIP is $35 — and it includes one hour of early entry, line-skipping, reserved seating, and a cat ear headband souvenir — but VIP is limited to 300, so that one's worth grabbing now.

Norris Penrose is at 1045 Lower Gold Camp Rd in Colorado Springs. Full ticket info and a breakdown of all the activities are in our article below. Are you taking anyone to Cat Fest this year?

---
facebook.image_caption: Cat Fest Colorado is coming to Norris Penrose in COS on July 25 — cat show, adoption booths, costume contest, and more 🐱 General admission starts at $20 (under 5 FREE). Full guide linked.
